title: Loan Fest 2026: Apply for a Bajaj Finance Personal Loan Online with Attractive Offers
description: Pune (Maharashtra) [India], July 23: Bajaj Finance, one of India's leading financial services providers, has announced the launch of Loan Fest 2026, a limited-period campaign designed to make online borrowing more rewarding for eligible customers. Running from 10th July to 31st August 2026, the campaign enables customers to apply for a Bajaj Finance Personal Loan through a simple digital process while offering attractive rewards on successful loan disbursal. Alongside the campaign benefits, customers can access loan amounts ranging from Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 55 lakh, flexible repayment tenure of 12 months to 108 months, competitive personal loan interest rate options ranging from 10% to 30% per annum, and quick access to funds.

title: Rupee hits two-month low, oil prices remain focal point
description: The Indian rupee hit a two-month low on Monday as oil prices hovered around ninety dollars per barrel. In an effort to stabilize the currency, the Reserve Bank of India intervened by unloading dollars into the market. Meanwhile, Asian currencies and stock performances were varied, with the dollar index holding steady. This rise in energy prices could have implications for the Federal Reserve's future interest rate strategies.

title: How rural India is bearing the brunt of the inflation rise
description: India’s rural inflation has moved closer to 5%, while the urban rate remains below 4%. The gap is usually explained by higher food prices, but the latest data suggest price pressures may be spreading beyond food.
content: Rural inflation typically accelerates when food prices are rising or remain elevated. Food and beverages account for 23.2% of the rural basket, compared with 13.5% of the urban basket. With an uneven monsoon and the risk of deficient rainfall raising... [5377 chars]

title: RBI Governor flags Iran war, weak monsoon as the biggest risks to India’s inflation and economic growth
description: Elevated inflation persists due to West Asian tensions and a weak monsoon outlook. The Reserve Bank of India maintains its policy rate, diverging from global peers. Governor Malhotra stated that domestic inflation pass-through has remained limited so far. India's economic fundamentals remain strong despite geopolitical uncertainties and growth forecasts. Policymakers will monitor inflationary pressures before considering any monetary policy adjustments.

This initiative aims to attract foreign exchange until September 2026.
